posts.title: ©Public Image
posts.description: A landing page based on an interactive timeline to browse the studio's work.

posts.title: 100 Gecs
posts.description: So... what's that? I honestly don't know. But I just love when music bands get themselves crazy websites.
posts.credits: Ezra Miller, A Lot of Moving Parts

posts.title: 100k Studio
posts.description: Smooth interactions and unusual layout for this portfolio website of a Web Dev & Design website.
Artist/Furniture Designer Bryon Pritchard's site. Tilt your phone to drop logo letters with gravity or spin them with a swipe of your fingers.
Effortless and colorful table-based layout for an artistic residency in Berkeley, CA.
Jackpot on this e-commerce website. That's rare! Nice interface, great content, and we just love the products too.

posts.title: A Black Sun
posts.description: Bathe in the black sun with this immersive ambient poem.

posts.title: A proposal for immersive video on the web
posts.description: Interesting use of an interface to make a case and show in situ how to improve video players on the web, making them more interactive.

posts.title: A Scroll of One's Own
posts.description: Poetry on/with/through a website. Net.art on a smartphone.

posts.title: A year of Conscious Practice
posts.description: A very well-designed mobile website to read 12 essays written throughout a year of dialogue.

posts.title: A-COLD-WALL AUTUMN WINTER 2021
posts.description: Tactile website showing the AW21 lookbook, with glitch effects and sound design that reacts to browsing movements.

posts.title: ABC Dinamo
posts.description: Explore this vibrant, clean type studio site with smooth interactions. Navigate through typefaces and test them with your own text.

posts.title: ABRA
posts.description: Retro interface for this footwear brand.

posts.title: Acil & Pierre
posts.description: Parisian graphic designers who've made themselves a cool branding.

posts.title: Add seoul
posts.description: Have you ever petted a website? The interaction when touching the homepage's image is so satisfying that it's actually quite difficult to enter in the website.

posts.title: Alex Heeyeon Kill
posts.description: Smart yet light interface using the metaphor of a stack of documents to let you explore a writer's works.

posts.title: Alicia Mersy
posts.description: Insane graphic contents meshed together on this fun multi-layers website.

posts.title: Alt Text as Poetry
posts.description: Nice website for a project which invites exploring alt-text in a poetic way. Love the simplicity of the interface.
posts.credits: Bojana Coklyat Finnegan Shannon Laurel Schwulst Taichi Wi

posts.title: Amanda Yeh Piano Scrollbar Piano
posts.description: One of Amanda Yeh's projects and code experiments, scroll to play piano.

posts.title: American Haiku
posts.description: American Haiku, a new kind of creative agency from Brooklyn, NY

posts.title: And Festival
posts.description: Firstly pick a rendering quality to select how much data you'd like to load, then discover the festival's programme through navigating a 3D map.

posts.title: And Shy Magazine
posts.description: Online literature magazine exploring digital reading experiences, that features brilliant typographical interactions.
posts.credits: Katharina Nejdl, Sophia Rohwetter, Victor Kümel, Chris Möller

posts.title: Andrea Perato
posts.description: A google sheets iframe portfolio.

posts.title: Andrew Benson
posts.description: Andrew Benson is an artist based in LA. His website is definitely not mobile friendly, yet it looks very cool on smartphone, so here we are.

posts.title: Angus Chiang Online
posts.description: Colorful e-commerce featuring a playground homepage with draggable elements.
posts.credits: An Yen, Angus Chiang, David Peñula

posts.title: Animals As Objects
posts.description: This website is the result of a research from Zoological Natural History Museum in Berlin, using a word cloud to browse the content.
posts.credits: Son-La Pham Rasmus Emanuel Svensson Claire Tolan

posts.title: Anna Ley
posts.description: Very cute and unusual navigation which lets you spin a wheel to browse the artist's works.

posts.title: Another Kind Image Archive
posts.description: Navigate through a vortex of archive images and videos.
posts.credits: João Drumond, James Chester, Luís Ferreira

posts.title: Apple Airpods Pro
posts.description: Is this Elon Musk's new spaceship? Ha, no. Bluetooth headphones. Without doubt, Apple's web-designers are watching space movies for inspiration.

posts.title: Arka
posts.description: Arka is a video platfom dedicated to architecture. The interface is simple and has cute transitions that feel a bit like early 2000s Flash websites.

posts.title: Arroyo
posts.description: Let's call this a digital travel diary. Jon Kyle texts us during his journey throughout LA, sharing thoughts and snapshots along his walk.

posts.title: Art House by The Webster
posts.description: Superficial.studio got inspired by Yves Klein and David Hockney to design fantasy virtual spaces with 3D interactive products.

posts.title: Art&Code8
posts.description: Anti-user-friendly exhibition website with frictional navigation to encourage visitors to engage consciously.

posts.title: Arthur Teboul
posts.description: Tap and draw with content.

posts.title: Ash Work
posts.description: Play with the focus of a CGI image.

posts.title: Atomo Management
posts.description: Another beautiful canvas experiment by Bureau Cool. Visual content are melted together, and create an infinite stream.

posts.title: Audiodrag
posts.description: This is a portfolio for an audio production studio. So, it just features audio players and that's it.

posts.title: AWGE
posts.description: A$AP Rocky's creative agency. Is this a retro video game? A vintage flash website? an old TV show made interactive? Can't say. Whatever you call it, it's brilliant.

posts.title: Axis Mundi
posts.description: Agency website for cultural research and semiotics studio Axis Mundi

posts.title: Badbadbadbad
posts.description: Bráulio Amado's website starts with a big

posts.title: Balenciaga Lookbook Videogame
posts.description: A collection shot using 360 views of each look. Campaign video also worth a watch (a recording of the collection's online video game that unfortunately went offline).

posts.title: Be Gen 21
posts.description: A personalised brief generator. Use it purposefully.

posts.title: Be Rude
posts.description: Cool/Messy design. Surprising hidden interactions to alter navigation – although you kind of end up not knowing what you're doing. But that's okay.

posts.title: Below the Surface
posts.description: Take a dive into the history of Amsterdam with this timeline navigation and discover the most extraordinary artefacts that have been excavated.

posts.title: Bids for Survival
posts.description: An immersive experience that offers a dive within a photogrammetric world, guided with voiceovers and and cut-scenes.

posts.title: Bleached
posts.description: Single page to promote a collective zine with a 3D vizualisation of the publication.

posts.title: Blend
posts.description: Mesmerizing experiment by No Plans, which lets you create colored bubbles that blend together.

posts.title: Blink
posts.description: Blink to reveal a message from a friend

posts.title: Blob Opera
posts.description: Impressive and fun interface which lets you play with singing 3D blobs. Opera singers have trained a machine learning algorithm to sing to create this.

posts.title: Bloomberg 2020 Best Books
posts.description: Bloomberg article with a fun interactive header that let's you play with all the books covers.

posts.title: Bon Iver Viisualiizer
posts.description: Discover Bon Iver's new album through this living breathing visualizer.

posts.title: Bong
posts.description: Toy around with BONG logo, which includes planet earth.

posts.title: Boring Conversation
posts.description: Chat bot designed to facilitate boring conversations.

posts.title: Bouquet
posts.description: Bouquet delves into the nature of a digital entity by exposing its data infrastructure.

posts.title: Breathe Resting Computer
posts.description: This experiment explores breath as a uniting force. Visitors breathe into their phone microphones, creating a colored orb that symbolizes their presence on the site, capturing the simplicity of human connection.

posts.title: Bridging the Zoomer-Boomer divide
posts.description: Blog article designed as a DM thred between the author and his friend.

posts.title: Bureau Cool
posts.description: Single-page yet rich portfolio for digital works. Starts with a 3D floating circle that displays recent visitors' location.

posts.title: Bus Group
posts.description: Cool portfolio interface for a cool CGI studio. Feels like browsing some kind of new Instagram interface for trendy graphic designers.

posts.title: Business Fonts
posts.description: Fun website to promote a font, featuring a text editor to try it live.

posts.title: Busy Simulator
posts.description: Such a BRILLIANT idea. A website that triggers apps notifications sounds, to make people think you're so busy.

posts.title: Cameron's World
posts.description: A web-collage of text and images excavated from the buried neighbourhoods of GeoCities.

posts.title: Canoa Lab
posts.description: Canoa Lab’s ceramics come alive as images glide in a fluid, figure-eight motion.

posts.title: Carolee Schneemann Foundation
posts.description: Just very great graphics, with nice gradient animation that makes the UI

posts.title: Cash by Cash App
posts.description: Explorative game-like 3D experience that celebrates the ever-evolving diversity of the natural world.

posts.title: ccamera
posts.description: A photo app that doesn't allow new pictures to be taken. The website triggers your camera, you take a pic. Then, the website swaps it with a visually similar pro-existing photo from the web.

posts.title: CCI Graduate Showcase
posts.description: A fun juxtaposition of digital aesthetics and natural behaviours which lets you create ripples in pixels.

posts.title: Centrer for Planetary Pedagogies
posts.description: Cute minimal forest dances with the movement of your cursor.

posts.title: Chapps Online
posts.description: Click and drag to ping necklaces in a satisfying interaction for Emily Chapps' portfolio

posts.title: Cheap URLs Loan
posts.description: Retro leaflet vibes promoting affordable, fast, and convenient websites. A super minimal one-pager with a clever 'verso' detail. We're sold—take our money!

posts.title: Chiara Rigato
posts.description: Colorful portfolio with bold types scrolling horizontally. And a floating smiley face.

posts.title: Christie Wu
posts.description: Crazy interactive portfolio website with retro web design aesthetics.

posts.title: Christopher Melgram
posts.description: Feels like scrolling within a 3D box.

posts.title: Circuits of Truth
posts.description: Beautifully designed research project website about

posts.title: Claudia Rafael
posts.description: Claudia Rafael is a Digital Artist. Her website presents portraits created with A.I., which look like paintings.

posts.title: Cloakroom Botter
posts.description: Fullscreen interface that lets you browse BOTTER's collection through dressing up an underwater 3D avatar model.

posts.title: Cloudwatching
posts.description: A participative cloudwatching experience where the visitor can scroll through the skyscape or add to it by drawing their own cloud.

posts.title: Collection of Holiday Experiences
posts.description: Interactive experiences celebrating Xmas magic, eg. a baking cookies challenge or a decorating a tree drawing tool.

posts.title: Color Push : Wetransfer x Liebermann
posts.description: Impressive drawing tool to create a collaborative artwork for WeTransfer. You can either paint with your finger or enable camera to paint with your head.

posts.title: Colour Mapper
posts.description: Click anywhere to begin a real-time polar colour map

posts.title: Come Shave
posts.description: Fancy shaving a website? There you go. Watch out, digital hair's regrowth is quick!
posts.credits: Studio Moniker, Alexandra Barancova, Jae Perris

posts.title: Communal Radio Club
posts.description: Listen to live radio stations and enjoy quirky Web 1.0-style animations by clicking the blue jog dial. Leave your name on the listener list to mark your presence.

posts.title: Commune
posts.description: Simple colourful page introducing an online lecture program to the lucky students of Klasse Digitale Grafik.

posts.title: Consecutives
posts.description: Incredible text-based animation, waving and flowing, one of Damon Zucconi's artwork.

posts.title: Contents
posts.description: Just tap to trigger strobostropic stream of images. Effortless interaction that illustrates well Contents' job –

posts.title: Control The Virus 03
posts.description: Online exhibition bringing you some digital artworks. Feels like floating in a rainbow cotton nest.

posts.title: Coralie Wipf
posts.description: Colour swatches as a clever navigation system.

posts.title: Cormio
posts.description: Minimalist yet unconventional e-shop: just a grid of products and product page with big buttons, and a fullscreen pic in the background.

posts.title: Country Music
posts.description: A feed of music playlists with strong graphics illustrating each track.

posts.title: Cupidku
posts.description: Upload a picture of someone you cherish and Cupidku will generate a haiku love poem based on the image.

posts.title: Daisy Chain Studio
posts.description: Portfolio for Daisy Chain Studio with a cute stretchy flowers scrollbar

posts.title: Dance Party
posts.description: An unlikely cast of characters tearing up the third dimension.

posts.title: Daniel Givens
posts.description: Paint and distort glowing stripes that ripple with vibrant, psychedelic colors. So satisfying!

posts.title: Design your own hole
posts.description: A website as a toy! A touchable interactive HTML object.

posts.title: Desktop FM
posts.description: Teaser site where users can interact with 3D visual of a desktop.fm CD to play dreamlike musical chords.

posts.title: Developments
posts.description: Sleek mobile-friendly interface, which lets you browse interviews as in a slideshow, slide by slide.

posts.title: Dice
posts.description: Interactive poster with a 3d blob shape which reacts to the scrolling movement.

posts.title: Dictionary of Online Behavior
posts.description: Ever-growing illustrated lexicon of words related to the digital world. The index interface mimics Safari

posts.title: Diffusion Cam
posts.description: diffusion.cam, the first artificial social network where real humans share synthetic media. Use the camera to transform your photograph into text, which gets turned back into an image using an AI diffusion model.

posts.title: Dinamo Typefaces
posts.description: Cute and colorful website presenting fonts by Dinamo Typefaces in a playful and sleek UI.

posts.title: Drawing Garden
posts.description: Gardening, but with emojis and less time.

posts.title: DSR NY
posts.description: An architectural portfolio with a Tetris-like layout and a flexible logo that moves as you scroll.

posts.title: Duuu
posts.description: Minimalist and grid-based design for an online radio.
posts.credits: Spassky Fischer Frédéric Jaman Manon Bruet Quentin Creuzet

posts.title: Edgar Schmitz
posts.description: Beautiful interaction. Drag your fingerprint on the interface to discover the work.

posts.title: Elastic Grid
posts.description: Drag the screen to stretch and pinch an elastic checkered grid. Tap to switch up the layout.

posts.title: Elle's Homepage
posts.description: Welcome to Elle's under-construction homepage with a nostalgic 2000s vibe—click around and watch the pixel art character explore <3

posts.title: Emoji To Scale
posts.description: A guy just decided he would create a website to classify emojis based on the actual scale of represented objects.

posts.title: Endless Horse
posts.description: A horse that never ends.

posts.title: Entire Studios
posts.description: Immersive e-shop page that includes sound and animations to enter in the brand's world.

posts.title: ertdfgcvb
posts.description: Enter the matrix with this experimental micro website.

posts.title: Estudio Piedras
posts.description: Puzzle interaction to play with the studio's branding and content.

posts.title: Everything Can Be Scanned
posts.description: everything.can.be.scanned (e.c.b.s) — is a project that allows you to see basic objects, which usually we don’t pay much attention to and don’t even notice them existing, in a more detailed way.

posts.title: Ezekiel Aquino
posts.description: Ezekiel not only coded a generator for piano impromptus tracks, but also found a beautiful way to play with the scores' display.

posts.title: f451.faith
posts.description: A temporary website as an online raffle to bet on the release date of the actual website.

posts.title: Fast Cash Money
posts.description: A fake cryptocurrency website that fits into a larger fictionalized network of websites (2018).

posts.title: Feeld for Play
posts.description: Fun game to promote a dating app. Turn on your camera to generate your 3D floating shape that will maybe flirt with other online users.

posts.title: Feline Align
posts.description: Very addictive (and frustrating) 3D puzzle. Btw, don't worry if you can't make it, it's actually very challenging.

posts.title: Felipe Pantone: Contactless
posts.description: Art exhibition proposing manipulable sculptures and paintings, adapted from the artist's 'IRL' pieces.

posts.title: Finger Walking
posts.description: Cute website that invites you to mimic walking with your fingers to browse the page

posts.title: FLIP
posts.description: Just flip your phone.

posts.title: Floating In Entropy
posts.description: An ever-evolving virtual fashion collection.

posts.title: Floris Schrama
posts.description: The layout seems quite simple at first sight, but then there's a cool 3D transition to swap categories.

posts.title: Foam Talent
posts.description: Discover photographers by orbiting their visuals in 3D.

posts.title: For ever ware
posts.description: This single page works as an interactive poster, click RESET to randomise the illustration.

posts.title: Franky's Hats
posts.description: A stylish e-commerce site blending modern-retro design with textured visuals and an AR try-on feature.

posts.title: Frederic Briolet Monolith
posts.description: Beautiful WebGL experiment by Frederic Briolet, including an interactive 3D scene reacting with sound.

posts.title: Fruitful school
posts.description: Fruitful school teaches code to people. Their website nicely combines retro/default HTML elements and fruits emojis.

posts.title: Fuck Investments
posts.description: Fine art gallery website

posts.title: Fuse
posts.description: Cool interface and cool content: FUSE is a listing of links to discover digital entertainment references.

posts.title: Fuse.kiwi – Interesting Internet
posts.description: Collection of digital references, with new links posted weekly that disappear after 7 days.

posts.title: Future Core
posts.description: Discover the stunning Futrecore 2000 dance piece with its website exploring stroboscopic effects and the interplay between organic and techno elements.

posts.title: Gegenwart
posts.description: Single-paged website with randomized theme which gives you the feeling of scrolling within a 3D box.

posts.title: General Index
posts.description: A simple e-commerce website introduced by an animated graph.

posts.title: Giga Stock
posts.description: Giga Stock works as a cinematic vitrine for advertising a limited production of serialised works.

posts.title: Glitch Mcconnell
posts.description: Create cursed glitch art with Mitch McConnell

posts.title: Glob
posts.description: Fun e-shop including an interactive slideshow that lets you distort the lookbook.

posts.title: Glossary of Time
posts.description: An incomplete glossary of time. Exploring the gaping holes in the lexicon of time that we don’t even realise we are missing.

posts.title: Glossary of Undisciplined Design
posts.description: Very cool interface for this collection of disobedient projects. Navigate through the categories using folders.

posts.title: gmbh
posts.description: Minimalist e-commerce with non-conventional structure and sharp native buttons, giving it an industrial style.

posts.title: Good Glyphs
posts.description: This website presents a dingbat font. Its interface lets you try it online and looks like a poster.

posts.title: Gravity Studio
posts.description: Meet gravity employees in this playful, lo-fi take on a dress up game.

posts.title: Greg Roque
posts.description: Nothing conventional here. Greg Roque welcomes you on his webpage with a fullscreen video of himself yawning.

posts.title: Groove with me
posts.description: Pick a character and make it groove within this online fantasy land.

posts.title: GT Flexa
posts.description: You actually don't even have to interact with this website as it's having fun by itself. Just sit and watch.
posts.credits: Grilli Type, Sensor Station, Informal Inquiry,

posts.title: GT Maru Typeface
posts.description: Very cute website introducing GT Maru font's background and history through animated illustration triggered on scroll.
posts.credits: Grilli Type, Josh Schaub, Sensor Station

posts.title: Gucci Dive
posts.description: Impressive WebGL game which works very smoothly on mobile. Clever use of smartphone's gestures in the gameplay.

posts.title: Gucci Gift 2017
posts.description: A mix of digital paintings, interface elements and animations presenting Gucci's 2017 selection of gifts.

posts.title: Gucci Mascara Hunt
posts.description: Entertaining 3D pinball game with smooth interactions.

posts.title: H0t House
posts.description: Playful interactions and a smart theme-based search tool for exploration.

posts.title: Hackathon FWB
posts.description: Web3 hackathon website with a cool graphic layout.

posts.title: Haegeman Temmerman
posts.description: Exhaustive portfolio with tidy navigation through native dropdown menus.

posts.title: HAHA Services
posts.description: Playful touch interaction.

posts.title: Haiku Haiku
posts.description: Poetic and colorful website which offers collaborative haiku writing – either start a new haiku or complete another author's.

posts.title: Half of 8
posts.description: Website for collective half of 8. Features an infinite grid for exploring chapters of the ‘Half of Journal’ zine, a music player, and even a narrative expedition of Mt. Fuji!

posts.title: Hallo internet
posts.description: Nice bold imputs for this website that gathers a large collection of contemporary websites, all wrapped in desktop devices.

posts.title: Han Kjøbenhavn
posts.description: Interesting layout concept for this e-commerce that explore the aesthetics of google sheets.

posts.title: Harley Weir
posts.description: This website presents interactive 3D scans of Harley Weir's ceramics pieces. One of them is a lady bust that speaks and welcomes you on the website.

posts.title: Harry Boyd
posts.description: Interacting with elastic shapes on mobile is very satistifying.

posts.title: Headline Vision
posts.description: Latest news headlines as seen through a generative image AI, with nice glitched transitions.

posts.title: HFBK Raumplan
posts.description: Fun website presenting the Design course of University of Fine Arts of Hamburg.

posts.title: Holen Und Bringen
posts.description: Navigate through this dense informational website enhanced by a simple navigation and nice use of typography.

posts.title: Holodec World
posts.description: A mesmesizing landing page introducing Holodec's new tracks. It feels like a painting that moves and also has sound.

posts.title: Hudson Mohawke
posts.description: Fun early 2000s Windows XP user interface vibe, including a rude Clippy character (the paperclip guy on Microsoft Word).

posts.title: Hudson Mohawke - CHIMES RMX
posts.description: Drag'ndropping Hudson Mohawke's face through the galaxy, while listening to his new soundtrack. Sound like a plan.
posts.credits: International Magic, Warp Records, Hudson Mohawke

posts.title: Hybrid by Nature
posts.description: Scroll down to dive further into this psychedelic 3D funnel that acts as a digital leaflet, introducing artists featured in Hybrid by Nature, a media art project.

posts.title: Hybrid Vigor
posts.description: When picking 2 organisms, their settings are blend to create a new set of 6. So you start with a very diverse set and end up with more and more similar shapes.

posts.title: Igloo Inc.
posts.description: Super-smooth scroll-activated website for igloo inc. with beautifully clean 3D renderings and cool sound design

posts.title: Image Morph
posts.description: Use accelerometer to play with this interactive illustration.

posts.title: Imaging Big Numbers
posts.description: An experience to help visitors conceptualize big numbers through interactive visuals and thoughtful text.

posts.title: In Common With
posts.description: Simple, elegant interaction turning the lights off and on for this lighting brand.

posts.title: Indirect Flights
posts.description: An online work by artist Joe Hamilton. Just let your swiping gesture wander through an infinite landscape of layered materials.

posts.title: Infringe
posts.description: Digital version of Infringe, a magazine celebrating the art of hair. Bold font system.
posts.credits: New Studio, Dimitri Papazoglou, Axel Peemöller

posts.title: Insight Agency
posts.description: An interactive magnifying glass lets you explore a grid to preview Insight Agency's photography portfolio.

posts.title: Intimacy Machine
posts.description: A creative way to explore a layout; by sliding a magnetic grid, you get access to a highly specific research data.

posts.title: Iota Editions
posts.description: Simple website with a fun navigation: to open a category, instead of a basic tap, you'll need to drag from one corner to another. It somehow provides a materiality sensation.

posts.title: iPod.js
posts.description: Feeling nostalgic?

posts.title: ISHI MAG
posts.description: A tangkhul, community led magazine. Exploring various indigenous experiences based out of Ukhrul, Manipur (India).

posts.title: Issued by Bottega
posts.description: This online magazine has been launched by Bottega after the brand has left IG.

posts.title: It's a Book
posts.description: An abstract interpretation of a bookshelf as an interactive grid.

posts.title: Jan Kaps
posts.description: Beautiful bookstore website which lets you browse its collection by floating around a sphere.

posts.title: Jana Reddemann
posts.description: Pure default HTML aesthetics.

posts.title: Jazz Keys
posts.description: Send a message in Jazz!

posts.title: Johanna Jaskowska
posts.description: Johwska is a 3D artist who mainly creates AR filters. This website introduces her approach.

posts.title: Joseph Pleass
posts.description: Let yourself be guided through this fun portfolio thanks to cinematic subtitles.

posts.title: K2design Calendar
posts.description: Dragging a typographical circle will change the HSB of colours in this 3D Digital Shop for k2design's 2023 Calendar.

posts.title: Kilbi 2020
posts.description: Browsing this Swiss music festival feels like landing on Kilbi's galaxy.
posts.credits: Katharina Reidy, Adeline Mollard, Thibault Brevet

posts.title: Kim Wermes
posts.description: Simplistic yet fun tear-off flyer website for Kim Wermes

posts.title: Klimaschau
posts.description: Digital exhibition with an interesting browsing as if scrolling through a map.

posts.title: Kris Ludi
posts.description: A text as a website.

posts.title: Ksenia Schnaider
posts.description: Fashion e-comm website with nice UI details, and cool aesthetics.

posts.title: L'inconnue
posts.description: Poetic and minimalist website for this contemporary art Gallery with anamorphic homepage.

posts.title: Labyrinth
posts.description: Super cute little game created with colorful textures made of drawings by 10-years-old kids.

posts.title: Lachlan Bailey
posts.description: Portfolio site for Lachlan Bailey with simple UI and nested project info.

posts.title: LCF SMC Show 21
posts.description: Discover London College of Fashion 2021's graduates projects through browsing this 3D virtual exhibition.

posts.title: Leila Mcglew
posts.description: Playful yet simple page that lets you draw emojis everywhere. Satisfying!

posts.title: Liebermann Kiepe Reddemann
posts.description: Feels like trying to read a newspaper that you're holding upside down.

posts.title: Ling's Cars
posts.description: Thanks!

posts.title: Litkovskaya
posts.description: Beautiful e-commerce website that combines nice visual content + good graphics.

posts.title: Loading Giacomonanni
posts.description: A website that never stops loading.

posts.title: Lofi Cafe
posts.description: A radio that includes a TV screen to swich channels and that is actually displayed on a website.

posts.title: Loverboy
posts.description: 360 lookbook giving access to LOVERBOY's products.

posts.title: Lucy Hard Castle
posts.description: A poetic portfolio that welcomes you with a virtual/abstract 3D landscape.
posts.credits: Lucy Hardcastle Studio, DxR Zone, Haxiomic / George Corney, Jess Mae, Oli Easte

posts.title: Make Everything Ok
posts.description: Press the magic button to make everything ok.

posts.title: Make Word Art
posts.description: Go back in time with this nice web adaptation of the coolest typographic tool from the 2000s.

posts.title: Martine Rose
posts.description: A real-time experience of Martine Rose's SS23 show, looping perpetually.

posts.title: Max Bittker
posts.description: Max Bittker is an artist, a teacher and a coder. He's the creator of <a href=

posts.title: Messenger
posts.description: It's a small planet, but someone's gotta make the deliveries. Chill, lo-fi browser game with beautiful sound design.

posts.title: Micaiah Carter
posts.description: Discover Micaiah Carter’s debut monograph 'What’s My Name' on a visually striking website, featuring an interactive 3D book and animated pages floating in the background.

posts.title: Michael Speichert
posts.description: This website uses 3D objects as user interface elements. Sounds risky? Yes, although it works.

posts.title: Michal Turtle Reinterprated – Planisphere Editorial 2020
posts.description: An ever-evolving landscape to browse a music album.

posts.title: MIT's Center for Advanced Visual Studies
posts.description: The layout is simple, sharp. It starts casually, then as you scroll, all the page gets distorted, like if the interface was mapped to a 3D ribbon. Try it!

posts.title: Mobile HSL Picker
posts.description: Color Picker with gyroscope sensors

posts.title: model und ruine
posts.description: This german festival website brings a nice evolutive layering navigation.

posts.title: Molly Ellison
posts.description: A graphic designer's 360 portfolio that requires you to move you phone around to explore.

posts.title: Mono X7
posts.description: An infinite 3D scrolling gallery to explore artworks available on Mono X7's digital canvas.

posts.title: Mousing Around
posts.description: How does it feel to use a pointer cursor on a touch screen?

posts.title: Museum At Large
posts.description: Adjust your reading rhythm by navigating with a drawing tool.

posts.title: My Smol Site
posts.description: This is a site to help people make one-page sites, resurrecting Clippy in the form of the cartoon character from the Loki series.

posts.title: N10.AS
posts.description: N10.AS is an online radio station based in Monteral, they have a cool website with 90s GUI.

posts.title: Nahee App
posts.description: Artist/Teacher/Programmer Nahee Kim performs under the name “nahee.app”. This website is both an experiment and a portfolio, with a very novel navigation.

posts.title: Nam Huynh
posts.description: Nam welcomes you on his portfolio with a memory game that has 3 levels. Effortless and playful at the same time!

posts.title: Narrow Design
posts.description: Scroll through Nick Jones' portfolio, oriented around the golden ratio.

posts.title: Natalia Jordanova
posts.description: A list of works in presented with a 3D perspective. Your scrolling also spins a funny little wheel.

posts.title: Nearrest Neighbor
posts.description: Thesis virtual exhibition for UCLA students. They've brilliantly transitionned from an IRL to an online format.

posts.title: Neusicht
posts.description: Crystal clear informational website with an interesting layout and hidden game feature.

posts.title: New Aesthetic 2
posts.description: Playful single page introducing this new publication by Sorry Press, which makes you interact with the book while scrolling down.

posts.title: New Dawn
posts.description: Virtual exhibition with a cool digital identity.

posts.title: New Legibility
posts.description: Click through this interactive essay to uncover the full narrative.

posts.title: New York Times - This is 18 around the world
posts.description: Another example of NY Times's brilliant take on digital content. They certainly know how to immerse the reader in a story.

posts.title: Nicky Tesla
posts.description: Nicky's page is a shared spritesheet, on which you'll find all info a personal page requires + you can select a cell and fill it with a color. Fun, smart and simple.

posts.title: Nicolas Sassoon
posts.description: A website that uses pixels as fabric.

posts.title: Nike - Swoosh
posts.description: Loading page where users can interact with a 3D nike swoosh, made from particles which can be expanded and contracted on tap.

posts.title: Nike Moonshot
posts.description: A Moon generator, that creates a snapshot made of distorted circles.

posts.title: No Plans Show
posts.description: No Plans turned their portfolio into a digital exhibition. By stretching the video recordings of their interfaces, it turns them into an abstract material.

posts.title: Noko Studio
posts.description: Ultra lightweight, super simple studio landing page featuring a silly interactive logo experiment.

posts.title: Nold Boards
posts.description: Skateboarding brand site that gives you left or right menu depending on whether you are a 'regular' or a 'goofy'

posts.title: Nonymous
posts.description: They made the act of scrolling elastic and satisfying.

posts.title: Novembre Global
posts.description: Beautiful editorial website juggling between big type and big images

posts.title: Ntgent
posts.description: Website for NTGent, Ghent's city theatre.

posts.title: Odunpazarı Modern Museum
posts.description: Explore the museum's collection through a 360 navigation that gives you a glimpse of the building's wooden structures by architect Kengo Kuma.

posts.title: Of Hundred
posts.description: Online radio show that curates various range of sounds.

posts.title: Offline only
posts.description: An interface that embodies its message. You need to turn on plane mode to access this website and reveal a text explaning how disconnecting makes us able to pay attention.

posts.title: ONE
posts.description: Minimalist yet efficient website showcasing the work from ONE, a collective reuniting outstanding digital designers and 3D artists.

posts.title: oneiric
posts.description: Interact with a 3D cube to browse the magazine's categories.

posts.title: Online Ceramics
posts.description: Single page e-shop that use only default fonts, and primary HTML colors. Actually really wanna get one of these sweaters.

posts.title: Online Tea Timer
posts.description: Brew the perfect cup of tea with this online tea timer.

posts.title: Only Bags
posts.description: Cool minimal e-commerce with 3D spinning bags and a shopping trolley POV

posts.title: øøø
posts.description: Draw with multicoloured stamps, or with circles and lines that have a life of their own.

posts.title: Open Foundry
posts.description: Curation of free fonts that look cool, with a sleek interface.

posts.title: Opening Ceremony
posts.description: Cool e-commerce website with nice animated details: cloudy blue sky in the background, scrolling titles. We like that don't we?

posts.title: Orb Farm
posts.description: A cute and quirky aquatic organism invites you to interact with its retro environment, where you can nurture, sculpt and observe.

posts.title: Osk
posts.description: Discover the emotional potential of this navigation through a troubling video element and some various voiceovers.

posts.title: Oto Nove Swiss Paris
posts.description: This festival's website includes a music player with a beautiful yet minimalistic sound visualisation.

posts.title: Otoro
posts.description: Feed cute pets on this playful simple one pager.

posts.title: Paint.stx
posts.description: Create absolute chaos with psychedelic brushes.

posts.title: Paula De Álvaro
posts.description: De Álvaro questions the fragility of online protoflios with an absurd and ironic web-folio showcasing her printed instagram archive.

posts.title: Perlmutare
posts.description: Generating absurd demands, that testify to the loss of political left/centre/right orientation.

posts.title: Pics of People Taking Pics
posts.description: A gallery of people taking photos—meta moments that capture our photo-obsessed culture.

posts.title: Piyenji
posts.description: A gallery of iOS Stickers shown an iOS messaging interface.

posts.title: Pizza Pizza
posts.description: A website that feels like browsing an app. Smooth transitions, sleek design.

posts.title: Planet Digital
posts.description: An exploration of digitalization by the University of Zurich and Museum für Gestaltung, where a kaleidoscopic entry screen leads to a hyperlinked web of embedded artworks

posts.title: Plastic Air
posts.description: It's beautiful although meant to show us something not that cute.
posts.credits: Giorga Lupi, Talia Cotton, Phil Cox (Pentagram)

posts.title: Play Date
posts.description: Handle this console in a playful and fluid way in this simple one-pager.

posts.title: Pleasures
posts.description: Cyberpunk and late 90's inspired computer console with generative background and windows you can resize and rearrange.

posts.title: Pola - Mother's Day
posts.description: Cute animation triggered while scrolling down the page.

posts.title: Pond GG
posts.description: Log on this website and interact with other online users as a duck.

posts.title: PoolSide FM
posts.description: Travel trough this happy place with nostalgic graphics and retro sounds.
posts.credits: Niek Dekker, Aziz Firat, Pascal Pixel

posts.title: Predominantly
posts.description: This website bring an element of serendipity back into the search for music by use of color panel to navigate, making it a personal and delightful experience.

posts.title: Presented by Bottega
posts.description: Another fancy slideshow by Bottega, proving once more that they were so right to leave Instagram!!!!!!

posts.title: Public Possession
posts.description: Driven by the fun of creation, the record label Public Possession makes gifs pops-in as you navigate your way through its shop.

posts.title: Publication Studio
posts.description: Browsing clouds of floating publications.

posts.title: Rabbit Hole
posts.description: A tool for finding direction by getting lost, including visual compositions created with totally random data.

posts.title: Rachel Kim
posts.description: Rachel Kim's website definitely proves a portfolio doesn't have to include burger menus, slideshows and white backgrounds.

posts.title: Rachel Maclean
posts.description: Visit Rachel Maclean's colorful and playful world.

posts.title: Radical PR
posts.description: Effortless.

posts.title: Radicals Radicants
posts.description: This dense informational website envelopped its content in old school but delightful HTML frames.

posts.title: Radio Orsimanirana
posts.description: Online radio that acts as the digital platform linked to an exhibition. Pick a floating shape to select a program.

posts.title: Rauno
posts.description: Paint the tiled homepage with a swipe, then dive into Rauno’s portfolio through a clean, intuitive UI.

posts.title: Real Life
posts.description: Online magazine about living with technology. Simple and clean layout with an interactive background: article's imagery is blurred it to create a texture.

posts.title: Refix
posts.description: Solid big bold type nagivation to lure you in to explore further.

posts.title: Remember to Forget
posts.description: “Remember to Forget” by Faris Kassim archives 10 years of film slides, blurring images to the point of complete indistinguishability. Through a process akin to memory recall, the project downsizes and reconstructs images, filling in gaps with imagined data.

posts.title: Revision
posts.description: Colorful e-commerce featuring a playground homepage with draggable elements.

posts.title: Rifke World
posts.description: We love Rifke's simple website. You land there, it feels like there's nothing and eventually, your action reveals the content.

posts.title: Ring Variations
posts.description: Trippy three dimensional ring variations.

posts.title: Robopoetics
posts.description: Web-based exhibition for Internet poetry. It's beautiful, light and the browsing is very enjoyable and easy.

posts.title: Roel Kok
posts.description: Let typography melt into an iridescent puddle in this beautifully simple interaction by Roel Kok

posts.title: ROOM
posts.description: Strong visuals — pick a cover opens a page with 3D CD disk and music player of the selected track.

posts.title: Rude Captcha
posts.description: Because AI can't be rude.

posts.title: Saaaam
posts.description: Fun interaction where you can customise the colours and styling of shapes with a slider.

posts.title: Sabrina Zeltner
posts.description: Textured, layered portfolio website that feels like an interactive poster.

posts.title: Sacred Display
posts.description: This 3D gallery is called

posts.title: Sail Awway
posts.description: Beautiful sensitive page presenting Maria Fernanda Fuentes' dream pop music project.

posts.title: Samuel Day
posts.description: Sam's website is like an interactive comic strip: swipe down to browse whats within his head.

posts.title: Samuel Dumez
posts.description: Clever portfolio website where information is revealed through a crossword

posts.title: samuel Weidmann
posts.description: Cursor fireworks on click light up the horizon of this digital floating business card.

posts.title: Sandspiel
posts.description: Draw, it creates sand that falls with gravity, and then these particles make flowers grow. Or you pick lava and it burns everything. Just try it!

posts.title: Sans Sheriff
posts.description: So this website presents a sans-serif font and they've made a game with a sheriff character whom can be shot. Don't you get it?!!

posts.title: Scale to Infinity, Audrey Large
posts.description: 3D looks just amazing, interface is nicely done, interactivity and transitions are just great. I kind of wish I had designed that website tbh.

posts.title: Sce.e
posts.description: Experience experimental audio-reactive visuals where abstract shapes sync with minimal techno drum patterns, all crafted with JavaScript.

posts.title: Schauspielhaus Zürich
posts.description: Digital poster for the 2021/22 season of Schauspielhaus Zürich theater. Scroll down to the programme overview, then scroll back up!
posts.credits: Studio Laurenz Brunner & Clemens Piontek, Julia Novitch, Diana Pfammatter, Martin Andereggen

posts.title: Schultzschultz - ToucheType Tool
posts.description: Multi-touch interface for digital hand-lettering.

posts.title: Scrolling the Arcane
posts.description: Enter into introspection by using your phone's motion and camera sensors to pan round an otherworldly dome.

posts.title: Seen
posts.description: 3D world of super cute gaming controllers revealing text conversations about race, ethnicity and the creative industries.

posts.title: Self Titled Work
posts.description: Randomly generating color stories as abstract art, transforming the browser into a canvas.

posts.title: Selling Bricks
posts.description: Drag textured video snippets across your screen to explore an evolving architectural research project.

posts.title: Shang Xia
posts.description: Unusual navigation to explore the collection. Minimalist grid-system and sleek transitions.

posts.title: Simon Rogers
posts.description: A website as a ribbon.

posts.title: Simone's Computer
posts.description: Visiting Simone Mazulli's website from a mobile feels weird: as if Windows 95 was running on a 2020 smartphone. Huh!!

posts.title: Slava Kirilenko
posts.description: A portfolio website for type designer Slava Kirilenko that features a site-wide editable type explorer.

posts.title: Slingshot Camera
posts.description: Drag your finger to aim and take a snapshot of your surroundings.

posts.title: Smack dat ass
posts.description: You'll surprise yourself spending more time smacking asses than you initially expected.

posts.title: SMC REAL:TIME
posts.description: A platform reuniting projects by UAL students through several exhibition rooms.

posts.title: Snake
posts.description: Draw ur wish in the year of the snake for someone!

posts.title: Soft Power
posts.description: Great balance between an experimental interactive background and clean UX/UI.

posts.title: Somerset House’s Decentralise
posts.description: 'Decentralise' is an online platform which explore the history of Black British art, through engaging with 16 key objects from Somerset House exhibition.

posts.title: Son La Pham
posts.description: Son La's website is like a living ecosystem. Draw pixels and they'll start having a little digital life of their own, moving, growing, changing.

posts.title: Sona Stream
posts.description: Web3 music streaming service with a fun sinusoidal animation.

posts.title: Source Type
posts.description: A platform for typographic research and visual literacy. Super-smooth perspective scroll through their website content.

posts.title: Special Offer
posts.description: That's a bold statement: an image-less landing page for a portfolio. Yes, because the page's design says it all. Sharp.

posts.title: SR_A SR_A
posts.description: This studio's landing page has a playful diagram that lets you interact with floating thumbnails of their projects.

posts.title: SS21 Rains
posts.description: Virtual runway/lookbook interface that lets you switch camera to browse the looks for Rains SS21 collection.

posts.title: Stand in School for Graphic Design
posts.description: Website for the Stand in School for Graphic Design with playful gravity interactions.

posts.title: Staring Caring
posts.description: Dive into an ocean of colored shapes. There's nothing to 'do' except tapping to control the direction of your flow.

posts.title: Streaming Consciousness
posts.description: Experimental project presenting a stream of 47 anonymous young people's innermost thoughts.
posts.credits: Pitch Studios, Twomuch Studios, Nathalie de Valliere

posts.title: Stripe Press
posts.description: Scroll through a 3D virtual bookshelf to discover the publications released by Stripe Press.

posts.title: Studio Ānanda
posts.description: Channeling Studio Ānanda's spirit of wellness with soft use of CSS filters, blend modes, and SVG animations.

posts.title: Studio Runk
posts.description: Super playful and smart interface that makes you try screen printing online.

posts.title: Suddenly Rocks
posts.description: Cutout rocks as navigation – why not? Simple and elegant, smart UX, no fuss. Yes to this.

posts.title: Swiss Design Awards 2020
posts.description: Beautiful Swiss grid-based layout combined with funny not-that-Swiss menu design: a 3D carousel to explore categories.
posts.credits: Dual Room, Tristan Bagot, Swiss Design Awards, Anna Niederhäuser

posts.title: Taika x TWB
posts.description: A 3D product as an interface. Instead of reading a block of HTML text, just move around the 3D can to learn more about what this drink is about. Clever.

posts.title: Take a Selfie
posts.description: Fun experiment that creates a 3D Puzzle with the face camera's live video.

posts.title: temper
posts.description: In situ demo for a sleek one page website editor.

posts.title: The Art of Critique
posts.description: Single page that works as an interactive poster for this online exhibition. Including comprehensive program and details about the event.
posts.credits: Quentin Creuzet, Giulia Bierens de Haan

posts.title: The Emoji Personality Test
posts.description: The world-famous emoji-based personality test.

posts.title: The Internet Click
posts.description: Click the Internet companies before they reach the bottom to advance to the next level and avoid more ads.

posts.title: The Lost Lionesses
posts.description: Impressive experience that offers a dive within a 3D world, surrounded with voiceovers and films.

posts.title: The New Company
posts.description: Play around with gravity and draggable elements on this cheerful homepage.

posts.title: The Peach Spring Beyond This World
posts.description: Navigate an online exhibition through a 3D world, encountering works from two digital artists via text, links, and reaction videos

posts.title: The Road
posts.description: I know, it's just a wheel that makes noise. But just try it, it's kinda addictive! That could be the future of toys.

posts.title: The Sea Between Us
posts.description: Wander a pixel-art shoreline and find bottles carrying messages from others.

posts.title: The Wisdom of the Third Wave
posts.description: Rayon Vert and Slam Jam have joined forces to create this platform

posts.title: thisisneverthat FA22
posts.description: Thisisneverthat teaser site where your ASCII reflection is generated from brand typography.
posts.credits: Minhyung Lee, Jinkyu Song, Yeojin Jung, Hye Seob Shim

posts.title: Tienda - Design Store
posts.description: Rounded glowy buttons, colorful gradients and cute pictograms. A marketplace designed with a smart and playful mobile-friendly interface.

posts.title: Tight
posts.description: Immerse yourself in a OS system that becomes a tidy way of displaying a portfolio of work.

posts.title: Till Janz
posts.description: Fashion photographer with a background in UX Design and Programming. Hence, has a cool website.

posts.title: Tin & Ed
posts.description: Vibrant abstract 3D shapes that visitors can drag to 'paint' the screen with their trailing patterns.

posts.title: To See The Wave As A Sliding Door
posts.description: A website as a moving image, or digital poetry.

posts.title: Tom Gould
posts.description: Nice portfolio website using Instagram stories UX to browse projects content. Innovative way to avoid traditional slideshows!

posts.title: Too Busy Foundation
posts.description: Image archive shedding light on the hidden epidemic of human trafficking through embedded facts and statistics.

posts.title: Toph Tucker
posts.description: Press 'shake on' to have fun with the website's content, as if it was an elastic material that reacted to your movement.

posts.title: Trans Books
posts.description: Grid-based single page website with a fun inverted scrolling animation for the 2018 edition of this Japanese book fair.

posts.title: Two Much Rocks
posts.description: TwoMuch teamed up with illustrator Inga Ziemele to design this virtual garden. Pick and name your rock, then drop it on the grass among its peers.

posts.title: TwoMuch studio
posts.description: Play around with gravity and discover fantastic TwoMuch studio's work.

posts.title: uberraum
posts.description: Explore a surreal 3D planet where floating objects and busts of the design duo reveal hidden content, set to a captivating soundscape.

posts.title: United Fabric
posts.description: Spin the globe and click on names to receive messages from around the world.

posts.title: UNVEIL
posts.description: Elegant portfolio where scrolling through projects feels like flipping through a stack of records.

posts.title: Vàng Hoa
posts.description: The essence of what we call a 'Tatile Interactive Website'.

posts.title: Venice W.
posts.description: Enter into Venice Wanakornkul's phone to discover the world of her fashion brand.

posts.title: Vera VanDeSeyp
posts.description: Portfolio website with stretchy text and a guestbook where visitors can design their messages using a variable font.
posts.credits: Vera van de Seyp, Koos Breen

posts.title: Viennese Modernism
posts.description: Nice heavily gridded website with some 3D playful twists.

posts.title: Virgil Abloh™
posts.description: So this is Virgil Abloh's website and I hate saying this (!!!) but it looks great. Voilà.

posts.title: Viso Haus
posts.description: Land on a mesmerizing page of blurred objects leaving painterly light trails—drag to control their movement or tap to explore the portfolio

posts.title: Visual Society
posts.description: Neat Swiss shopping interface based on a filtering system: just check the content to display them on the page.

posts.title: Vivien Hoffmann
posts.description: Vivien Hoffmann is a graphic designer focusing on progressive typography. Her website has an unusual layout.

posts.title: Vlucendo Garden
posts.description: Enter a snow-covered ravine and drag your finger to plant succulents that bloom and fade.

posts.title: Volume of Light
posts.description: Photographer Thomas Brown offers you to adopt one of his images by assign it a title.
posts.credits: Thomas Brown Benedict de Silva Ben Weaver Studio

posts.title: Voth000
posts.description: Visually express your unique burnout journey by sharing thoughts and customising your avatar’s colours and graphic elements.

posts.title: Wandering Wondering Rocks
posts.description: A site and digitized cemetery of unanswered words – preserving what’s been left behind drifting, echoing, floating.

posts.title: Water Works Project
posts.description: Initiated by Field Records, Waterworks covers over 50 Dutch waterworks developed across Japan during the Meiji era. Navigate around the map and click on sites' names to learn about their development — often accompanied by visual or sonic impressions.

posts.title: Wear The Waste by H&M
posts.description: This H&M campaign offers a delicate browsing among the clouds. Smooth camera movements and sleak animations.

posts.title: Westminster Kennel Club
posts.description: Fun experiment that Google made when searching about this NGO dedicated to the sport of dogs.

posts.title: White Russian
posts.description: Portfolio interface that feels like an app.

posts.title: Wilderness Land
posts.description: Kristoffer offers us a map designed with a hijacked google sheet. Wander on the page to discover hidden links. 'The Internet is all around us, you live right in it.'

posts.title: Williams College Museum Of Art
posts.description: Perfect combinaison between smart UX and playful interaction. If only every museums could afford a website like that!

posts.title: Without Order
posts.description: Simple page which displays a watch, each time different when reloading.

posts.title: World On a Wire
posts.description: Tactile website, which feels like an interactive elastic poster to promote an artistic event by Hyundai Motorstudio.

posts.title: World Two
posts.description: Straight-forward interface to browse a stack of cards. Each card generated has an illustration and a title, randomly associated to question its meaning.

posts.title: Woven sound
posts.description: Use your touch to create your own musical and visual masterpiece.

posts.title: Xuan Ye
posts.description: Interactive 3D word cloud reacting when touched. Play with it: move around it or drag its intertwined elements.

posts.title: Yaeji
posts.description: Yaeji is cool. Her website is very cute and comes with a drawing tool.

posts.title: Yamauchi No.10 Family Office
posts.description: A playful isometric single-page for Nintendo’s Family. Navigate into a game screen with nice sound design and animated color blocks.
posts.credits: mount inc., 米道昌弘, 梅津岳城

posts.title: Yeezy Gap
posts.description: Dress an avatar in the Yeezy Gap x Balenciaga collection and use your phone’s accelerometer to catch doves.

posts.title: Yvon's
posts.description: fun and playful website selling bouncy castles, the shapes resemble the inflatables

posts.title: Zentrale
posts.description: A bookshop-inspired site where content is creatively framed like windows or book covers.

posts.title: Zes Nullen
posts.description: A cool e-commerce site where product shots seamlessly melt into each other as you scroll, creating a ‘crystal ball’ effect.

posts.title: ZOO
posts.description: The Pentagram team has designed a generative museum of curiosities for Zoo agency. It includes a bespoke soundtrack and a VIRTUAL ELEVATOR!
